heading: Setoff
label: 553 Setoff
notes: legislative statementsSection 553 of the House amendment is derived from a similar provision contained in the Senate amendment, but is modified to clarify application of a two-point test with respect to setoffs.

senate report no. 95–989This section preserves, with some changes, the right of setoff in bankruptcy cases now found in section 68 of the Bankruptcy Act [section 108 of former title 11]. One exception to the right is the automatic stay, discussed in connection with proposed . Another is the right of the trustee to use property under section 363 that is subject to a right of setoff.11 U.S.C. 362The section states that the right of setoff is unaffected by the bankruptcy code except to the extent that the creditor’s claim is disallowed, the creditor acquired (other than from the debtor) the claim during the 90 days preceding the case while the debtor was insolvent, the debt being offset was incurred for the purpose of obtaining a right of setoff, while the debtor was insolvent and during the 90-day prebankruptcy period, or the creditor improved his position in the 90-day period (similar to the improvement in position test found in the preference section 547(c)(5)). Only the last exception is an addition to current law.As under section 547(f), the debtor is presumed to have been insolvent during the 90 days before the case.

children.text: the claim of such creditor against the debtor is disallowed;

children.label: A
children.text: after the commencement of the case; or

children.label: i
children.text: after 90 days before the date of the filing of the petition; and

children.label: ii
children.text: while the debtor was insolvent (except for a setoff of a kind described in section 362(b)(6), 362(b)(7), 362(b)(17), 362(b)(27), 555, 556, 559, 560, or 561); or

children.label: A
children.text: after 90 days before the date of the filing of the petition;

children.label: B
children.text: while the debtor was insolvent; and

children.label: C
children.text: for the purpose of obtaining a right of setoff against the debtor (except for a setoff of a kind described in section 362(b)(6), 362(b)(7), 362(b)(17), 362(b)(27), 555, 556, 559, 560, or 561).

children.label: A
children.text: 90 days before the date of the filing of the petition; and

children.label: B
children.text: the first date during the 90 days immediately preceding the date of the filing of the petition on which there is an insufficiency.
In this subsection, “insufficiency” means amount, if any, by which a claim against the debtor exceeds a mutual debt owing to the debtor by the holder of such claim.

children.label: c
children.text: For the purposes of this section, the debtor is presumed to have been insolvent on and during the 90 days immediately preceding the date of the filing of the petition.
